title: Pentagon budget documents reveal it's pursuing containerized 300kW+ laser weapons, ambitious Joint Laser Weapon System designed to shoot down cruise missiles system part of $17.9 billion Golden Dome missile-defense initiative
summary: The Pentagon's FY2027 budget reveals a Joint Laser Weapon System, a containerized 300kW+ laser designed to intercept cruise missiles as part of the Golden Dome missile-defense initiative.
insight: Boosts US missile defense capabilities with high-tech laser technology

title: Talent over tokens: AI models are becoming more expensive to run, and productivity gains are limited efficient workers might be the solution to strained budgets
summary: Rising AI costs and per-token billing are making human workers more affordable alternatives, forcing companies to reevaluate their budgets and consider more efficient methods.
insight: Human labor costs may surpass AI efficiency in niche applications.

title: Pirate RPG game is secretly looting your SSD lifespan new Windrose patch promises smoother sailing and addresses excessive disk writing
summary: Gamers have reported excessive disk I/O from Windrose, consuming up to 30 MB/s of read/write activity. This excessive activity is causing concerns about SSD lifespan. The game's developers have promised a patch to address the issue.
insight: Excessive disk I/O shortens SSD lifespan significantly quickly

title: Crucial Taiwan undersea cable severed by old shipwreck backup microwave communications activated to keep population connected
summary: A shipwreck has severed a crucial undersea cable between Dongyin and Beigan islands, disrupting communication. The islands are strategically located near the Chinese coast and the northern mouth of the Taiwan Strait. Backup microwave communications have been activated to maintain connectivity.
insight: Taiwan's military presence in the islands is vulnerable to Chinese disruption

title: Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 and beyond, as HBM demand explodes customers already reserving supply years ahead, while the wider DRAM market begins to tighten
summary: Samsung and SK hynix warn of persistent AI-driven memory shortages until 2027 due to exploding HBM demand, which is overwhelming supply and tightening the broader DRAM market, leading to record profits.
insight: AI demand is outpacing memory supply, leading to prolonged shortages.

title: Newegg finally has a worthwhile Intel combo that includes memory, saving almost $240 snag an Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus, ASRock Z890 motherboard, and 32GB of GSkill Trident Z5 DDR5 RAM for only $768.34
summary: Newegg offers a discounted bundle including Intel Core Ultra 270K Plus, 32GB G.Skill Trident Z5 RAM, and ASRock Z890 Pro RS motherboard for $768.34, saving $239.98.
insight: Significant cost savings on high-end Intel build components available.

title: Sony confirms PS4 and PS5 digital games don't require an online check-in every 30 days new DRM policy only checks once for license to combat against refund scams
summary: Sony has clarified its digital game policy, confirming no 30-day check-ins are required. A single initial online check-in converts a temporary license to a permanent one. This alleviates concerns about frequent online connections.
insight: Single check-in for permanent license eliminates frequent online requirements

title: Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon capex spending to hit $725 billion in 2026, up 77% from last year analyst says bear thesis is 'garbage'
summary: Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta to spend $725 billion on capex in 2026, up 77% from last year.
insight: Big tech's massive capex surge fuels growth and innovation bets.

title: Victim of AI agent that deleted company's entire database gets their data back cloud provider recovers critical files and broadens its 48-hour delayed delete policy
summary: A SaaS business recovered its data after a cloud provider intervened, retrieving critical databases deleted by a rogue AI agent. The incident led to a revised 48-hour delayed delete policy. The cloud provider's swift action saved the company's operation.
insight: Cloud providers must act promptly to prevent AI-related data catastrophes.

title: Tennessee bans crypto ATMs that have become 'payment portal of choice for scammers' second state to restrict machines after Indiana
summary: Tennessee becomes the second state to ban crypto ATMs, following Indiana's lead, with the ban taking effect on July 1st. The legislation aims to restrict the use of these machines, which have become a popular choice for scammers. The ban is part of a broader effort to regulate the crypto industry.
insight: Regulating crypto ATMs reduces opportunities for financial scams.

title: Apple 'has given up' on Vision Pro, report claims costly price and weight behind purported failure, but the company continues to hire into its Vision Production Group
summary: Apple may be shifting focus away from Vision Pro due to price and weight concerns, but ongoing hiring suggests a reevaluation of strategy.
insight: Market expectations vs. product viability create a significant business challenge for Apple.

title: Get 32GB of RAM and 2TB SSD for $406 when paired with AMD's new 9950X3D2 Asus ROG X870E Apex gets you a monster overclocking AM5 bundle for $2,048, $400 off
summary: Get an AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 bundle with high-end RAM and storage for $2,048, with $398.98 off. The deal includes 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD, along with the Crosshair X870E Apex motherboard. This combo is available on Newegg.
insight: High-performance gaming PC bundle at a discounted price point

title: Homebrew PlayStation DualSense controller adapter for PC can be built for just $20 with a Raspberry Pi Pico wireless dongle delivers adaptive triggers and haptic feedback to gamers
summary: A PC gaming enthusiast has created a DIY adapter for the Sony DualSense controller, allowing Windows users to access its features wirelessly. The DS5Dongle utilizes a Raspberry Pi Pico wireless dongle to deliver adaptive triggers and haptic feedback. This adapter can be built for under $20.
insight: Wireless access to DualSense features on PC for under $20

title: Save $800 on this Alienware Aurora 5080 desktop 4K powerhouse with Intel Core Ultra 9 285K, 32GB of RAM, and 2TB of storage now just $2,899
summary: Get an Alienware Aurora 5080 desktop gaming PC with powerful Intel Core Ultra 9 285K processor, 32GB RAM, and 2TB storage for $2,899.
insight: Premium gaming desktop at reduced price for budget-conscious gamers.

title: Chinese GPU maker Cambricon's Q1 revenue hits $423 million as country's homegrown AI chip market accelerates Chinese chipmakers continue to leech market share from Nvidia
summary: Chinese chipmaker Cambricon's Q1 revenue reached $423 million as it gains traction in the country's AI chip market, further eroding Nvidia's market share.
insight: Chinese AI chipmakers accelerate market dominance over Nvidia

title: ESP-FLY DIY Kit is a tiny ESP32-S3-based DIY micro drone kit
summary: The ESP-FLY DIY kit is a miniature ESP32-S3-based drone kit ideal for STEM education and hobbyist projects. It features a compact quadcopter design, Wi-Fi 4, and Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity. The kit is available for $59.99 on Seeed Studio.
insight: Smallest ESP32 drone kit for STEM education and hobbyist projects emerges.

title: $4,290+ Unitree R1-A5 and R2-A7 humanoid robots features grippers or dexterous hands, fixed or wheeled base
summary: Unitree extends its R1 dual arm humanoid robot family with new R1-A5 and R1-A7 models, featuring grippers or dexterous hands and a fixed or wheeled base. These new robots can perform useful tasks with binocular vision. The R1-A7 model has longer arms and adds extra degrees of freedom.
insight: Humanoid robots gain utility with dexterous hands and mobility options.

title: NVIDIA phases out several Jetson modules due to high LPDDR4 RAM prices and tight supplies
summary: NVIDIA has accelerated the end-of-life timeline for several Jetson modules due to high LPDDR4 RAM prices and tight supplies. The impacted modules include the NVIDIA Jetson TX2 NX, TX2i, AGX Xavier, and Xavier NX. New purchase orders for these modules are now Non-Cancelable, Non-Returnable.
insight: LPDDR4 RAM shortages cripple NVIDIA Jetson module availability and pricing

title: Ubuntu is adding AI features, and it's on a collision course with its own user base
summary: Ubuntu is integrating AI features for accessibility and other tasks, potentially disrupting its user base.
insight: Canonical's AI integration may alienate core users who value simplicity and openness.

title: Meta contractor fires 1,100 AI trainers after they revealed Ray-Ban glasses recorded private and intimate footage
summary: Meta ended its relationship with contractor Sama after allegations of sensitive footage usage. Sama subsequently fired 1,108 employees, some of whom were punished for speaking out. The contractor had used footage from Ray-Ban smart glasses to train AI systems.
insight: Private data used for AI training raises ethics and accountability concerns

title: Security researcher just turned the PS5 into a Linux PC, and it can run GTA V at 60fps
summary: A security researcher has successfully turned the PS5 into a Linux PC, allowing it to run GTA V at 60fps. The process uses a patched vulnerability to boot into a Linux environment. However, the setup is not permanent and requires reloading Linux each time the console is rebooted.
insight: PS5 can now be repurposed as a high-performance gaming PC temporarily.

title: Microsoft releases the earliest DOS source code ever discovered as open source
summary: Microsoft has released the earliest known DOS source code as open source, known as 'Paterson Listings', providing scanned listings and transcribed portions. This significant piece of DOS history offers a glimpse into the development of the platform. The source code is now available for developers to study and learn from.
insight: Unlocking DOS history for developers and computer science enthusiasts

title: EU is considering new rules for cloud and AI under the Digital Markets Act
summary: The EU considers new rules for cloud and AI under the Digital Markets Act, targeting gatekeepers to prevent scale-driven competition blockage.
insight: Regulating tech giants to ensure fair competition and innovation in EU markets.

title: Early tests show minimal performance impact from new budget HUDIMM DDR5 memory
summary: Early tests show minimal performance impact from new HUDIMM DDR5 memory, suitable for gaming-focused systems.
insight: HUDIMM DDR5 maintains performance parity with dual UDIMM DDR5 setups.

title: Solar-powered cars are impractical at scale but this tiny build finds a sweet spot
summary: YouTuber Simon S rensen built a compact solar car using e-bike parts, keeping costs and complexity low.
insight: Repurposing existing e-bike tech optimizes solar car design and feasibility.

title: Google Photos feature uses AI to scan your pictures and help pick your clothes
summary: Google's Wardrobe feature uses AI to catalog clothes in Google Photos, creating a digital closet to help users choose outfits.
insight: Streamlining mornings with personalized outfit suggestions using AI-powered digital closet.

title: YouTube has quietly become the backbone of US classrooms
summary: YouTube has become a significant part of US classrooms, with students spending extensive time on the platform. Parents can access usage logs to see their children's screen time on school-issued devices. One seventh grader watched over 13,000 videos in just three months.
insight: Unregulated screen time hinders students' ability to engage with coursework.

title: Take-Two CEO says GTA 6 price will be "very reasonable" amid $100+ speculation
summary: Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick says GTA 6 will have a 'very reasonable' price tag.
insight: Price expectations set for Grand Theft Auto VI launch

title: AI data centers are delaying Texas housing projects by hiring away electricians
summary: Abilene builder Gene Lantrip's construction schedules are delayed due to AI campus hiring electricians, disrupting Texas housing projects.
insight: AI data center labor demand disrupts local housing construction timelines.

title: Google's AI bet is paying off: Alphabet posts $94.7 billion Q1 revenue, AI demand outpaces supply
summary: Alphabet posts $94.7 billion Q1 revenue, driven by Google Cloud's AI growth.
insight: AI demand surpassing supply drives Alphabet's revenue momentum.

title: Meta's AI bill keeps growing as spending forecast jumps to $145 billion
summary: Meta's AI bill is increasing due to higher component costs and data center investments. The spending forecast has jumped to $145 billion. This is attributed to CEO Mark Zuckerberg's efforts to support future capacity.
insight: Meta invests heavily in AI infrastructure for future growth potential.

title: Apple is giving up on the Vision Pro after weak sales and high return rates
summary: Apple has reportedly ceased development of the Vision Pro due to weak sales. The upgraded model failed to lift lukewarm sales, with only a few hundred thousand units sold. Production was dramatically cut after launching the AR headset in 2024.
insight: Apple's AR headset strategy faces significant commercial challenges ahead.

title: Xbox revenue falls again as hardware sales suffer another massive decline
summary: Xbox revenue falls 5% YoY, marking third consecutive quarterly decline. Microsoft attributes the drop to strong first-party game performance from the previous year. Hardware sales suffer a significant decline.
insight: Microsoft's Xbox revenue struggles underscore the gaming industry's volatility.
